channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.gamedeveloper.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.gamedeveloper.com

item.title: Switch 2 outpacing original Switch after nearing 20 million sales
item.content:encoded:
Chris Kerr,

Senior Editor, News,GameDeveloper.com

May 8, 2026

3 Min Read

The Nintendo Switch 2 has outperformed the Nintendo Switch during its first full fiscal year on shelves.

As noted in Nintendo's earnings report for the year ended March 31, 2026, the Switch 2 has sold 19.86 million units worldwide since launching in June 2025.

Nintendo said hardware sales were "more concentrated" over that launch period in comparison to previous systems. As a result, it now expects unit sales to decline year-on-year throughout FY27 (the fiscal year ending March 31, 2027).

"Reflecting strong launch-year sales and price revisions, we expect FY27 sales units to decline year-on-year. Even so, we believe this represents a solid level of adoption for Nintendo Switch 2 in its second year after launch," said Nintendo.

As the graph below shows, Nintendo is forecasting annual Switch 2 sales of 16.5 million units across FY27. 

That would still result in the console outperforming its predecessor over a 22 month launch window. Nintendo said the impact of its recently announced price hike for the Nintendo Switch 2 has been incorporated into that forecast.

Related:GameStop wants eBay, Nintendo confirms Switch 2 price hike, and union boss says EA buyout is a national security risk - Patch Notes #51

Switch 2 software sales totalled 48.71 million units across FY26. Nintendo expects that number to increase year-on-year to 60 million unis in FY27.

Discussing the performance of individual titles, the company revealed that Pokemon FireRed & LeafGreen cumulatively topped 4 million sales worldwide in six weeks despite being download-only. 

Pokemon Pokopia sold over 4 million units after five weeks on shelves, while Pokemon Legends Z-A Nintendo Switch 2 Edition delivered 3.94 million sales. 

Tomodachi Life: Living the Dream amassed more than 3.8 million sales two weeks after launching on April 16, 2026. Although it released as a Switch title, approximately 40 percent of players are Nintendo Switch 2 owners.

Donkey Kong Bananza is currently the second highest-selling Switch 2 release with 4.52 million unit sales, while Mario Kart World has retained its crown as the best-selling Switch 2 title with 14.7 million sales.

Nintendo said it attracted 129 million annual playing users across FY26—maintaining levels year-on-year. That metric includes the number of Nintendo Accounts that used Nintendo Switch 2 or Nintendo Switch software at least once during the 12-month data aggregation period. 

The Super Mario Galaxy Movie—the sequel to The Super Mario Bros. Movie—generated over $800 million in worldwide box office revenue within four weeks of hitting theaters.

"This movie is part of an initiative that [is] unique from our dedicated video game platform business, and we believe it is very significant in that it moves beyond the framework of video games, creating opportunities for even more people to experience Nintendo IP," said the company, discussing its transmedia efforts.

Related:Nintendo is raising Switch 2 prices around the world

"[...] Going forward, we will continue to create points of contact with consumers by promoting various initiatives that utilize Nintendo IP, working to convey the appeal of Nintendo to people of all ages around the world."

Across the entire fiscal year, Nintendo saw net sales increase by 98.6 percent year-on-year to 2.31 trillion yen ($14.75 billion). Operating profit increased by 27.5 percent to 360.1 billion yen ($2.3 billion) over the same period. 

The company is forecasting net sales of 2.05 trillion yen ($13.1 billion) and operating profit of 370 billion yen ($2.36 billion) across the next fiscal year ending March 31, 2027. 

About the Author

Senior Editor, News, GameDeveloper.com

Game Developer news editor Chris Kerr is an award-winning reporter with over a decade of experience in the game industry. His byline has appeared in notable print and digital publications including Edge, Stuff, Wireframe, International Business Times, and PocketGamer.biz. Throughout his career, Chris has covered major industry events including GDC, PAX Australia, Gamescom, Paris Games Week, and Develop Brighton. 



https://www.gamedeveloper.com/business/switch-2-outpacing-original-switch-after-nearing-20-million-sales
